ads.author: Michael Lafrey
ads.title: 1982 Gottlieb Devil's Dare Pinball Machine - Classic Widebody read description
ads.description: For sale is a cool 1982 Gottlieb Devil’s Dare widebody pinball machine — the first game to use Gottlieb’s improved System 80A board. This one has that awesome fiery red devil theme with bold artwork, flaming graphics, and a big yellow devil silhouette on the side. It’s a fun, fast-playing solid-state game with multiball modes and classic demonic speech (when it’s working). Condition highlights: • Glass (playfield and backglass) is in good shape — clear with no major cracks or haze. • Wood cabinet and side art are solid — no major damage or peeling. • Playfield lights up and the game plays mechanically. • Overall a great-looking piece with plenty of character. Needs service: • Some burnt-out bulbs (easy and cheap to replace with LEDs if you want brighter lights). • Speech/sound board is intermittent — sometimes works, sometimes doesn’t (common on these System 80A games; usually fixable with recapping, cleaning connections, or a board repair/rebuild). • Chrome on the legs is rusting (cosmetic — can be cleaned, painted, or replaced). This is a project machine perfect for a pinball enthusiast who enjoys tinkering. Once the sound/speech and lights are sorted, you’ll have a loud, flashy, and very playable classic from the early ’80s golden era of Gottlieb pinball. Price: $1,600 (or best reasonable offer — open to serious offers) Local pickup only (I’m in Riverview). No shipping — it’s a big widebody! Serious buyers only — please message with any questions. I can send more photos or videos of it powered on if you’d like. Cash and carry on pickup. Keywords: Gottlieb pinball, Devil’s Dare, System 80A, widebody pinball, arcade game, vintage pinball machine

ads.author: Matthew Haghighi
ads.title: DOUBLE DRAGON Arcade Cabinet – 1987 TAITO – Iconic Beat 'Em Up – Project / CRT / Original Wiring
ads.description: DOUBLE DRAGON Arcade Cabinet – 1987 TAITO – Iconic Beat ’Em Up – Project / CRT / Original Wiring Up for sale is an original Double Dragon arcade cabinet, one of the most important and influential arcade games ever made. Released in 1987 by Taito, Double Dragon is widely credited with defining the side-scrolling beat ’em up genre. This is the game that paved the way for classics like Final Fight, Streets of Rage, and countless others. It was one of the first true co-op brawler experiences, where two players could fight side-by-side through a continuous scrolling world—something that completely changed arcade gaming at the time. If you’re into arcade history, this is a big one. --- CONDITION / DETAILS: This is a project cabinet, and it’s priced accordingly—but it has a lot of the hard stuff already handled. Original CRT monitor – good working display New joysticks installed New buttons installed New control panel overlay New marquee Brand new side art package (cost me ~$200 alone) --- WHAT IT NEEDS: - Game PCB (board) is missing - Cabinet sides need: - sanding + finishing - Bondo work started on damaged corners but needs to be completed - Top panel is rough (see photos) --- WHY THIS IS A GREAT BUY: - Real 1987 Double Dragon cabinet – not a repro - One of the most iconic arcade titles ever made - Already has key upgrades done (controls, artwork) - Includes original CRT, not an LCD swap - Has correct wiring for an easy restoration - Perfect for: - Dedicated Double Dragon restoration - OR a JAMMA / multi-game build --- THE VIBE: This thing has that gritty, authentic late-80s arcade feel—the dragons wrapping the monitor, classic control panel artwork, and that unmistakable cabinet shape. Once finished, this will be a killer piece. --- BOTTOM LINE: This is a solid foundation project with a lot of money already put into parts and artwork. Finish it your way and you’ll have a legit, historically important arcade machine. Priced to sell.

ads.description: Edit March 20 We moved into new place and something happened along the way where it is no longer resetting. When you hit the reset button it makes a noise like it's going from one to two player but nothing resets. I know it's something minor. I have lowered the price considerably. Had every switch , blade and contact filed and cleaned and machine shopped out in 2021 by Terri Irwin , a long time respected pinball mechanic in Ocala. Has a few lights out. Only issue is sometimes the score reel does not completely set over and you have to hit the button again to restart it. One of those Ghost in the machine kind of things that she just couldn't figure out . I have owned this game since 1979 when I received it as a kid. You might have seen this up for sale in the past and then the listing disappeared. This was a period of time when I needed money for something so I put it up for sale but then something else came through and I took it down because I didn't really want to sell it. At this point in life it is mostly just nostalgia. I just don't play it anymore. It is very fun game I am just bored with it after 45 years and want something different. Very open to trades for a newer pinball machine plus cash on my end. Thanks for looking! Nine zero four four four four one four three nine.. Dave
